question_text: The Moon's gravitational field strength is about 1.6 N/kg, compared to Earth's 10 N/kg. An astronaut has a mass of 80 kg. Which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The astronaut's weight on the Moon is 80 N

options.label: B
options.text: The astronaut's weight on the Moon is 128 N

options.label: C
options.text: The astronaut's weight on Earth is less than on the Moon

options.label: D
options.text: The astronaut's mass changes when travelling to the Moon

explanation: Weight on the Moon = 80 kg × 1.6 N/kg = 128 N. Mass stays the same everywhere, but weight changes because gravitational field strength is different.
